purpose: Define and verify the release/tag provenance boundary so no release is considered canonical unless it is traceable to reviewed repository state, validation evidence, provenance metadata, and immutable artifact identity. Closes the remaining OPEN release/tag mutation path from the reverse-closure topology (RCM-016, RCM-017). Evidence only — no runtime route changes, no validator changes, no proof behavior changes, no authority creation, no deployment capability expansion.
scope: release provenance and artifact attestation boundary only

canonical_release_boundary.description: A release is canonical only if every step in this chain is satisfied and bound to the release artifact. Missing any step renders the release NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE or OPEN.

chain.name: pr_reviewed_commit
chain.description: The source commit was merged to main via a PR that passed CODEOWNERS review and required status checks. Direct push, force push, or unreviewed merge disqualifies the commit.
chain.current_status: OPEN — branch protection not enforced at GitHub settings level; depends on #380 closure

chain.name: required_status_checks
chain.description: The PR passed merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, and generate-sco-candidate before merge. Stale or missing status check evidence disqualifies the commit.
chain.evidence_required: PREO_VALID, SCO_CANDIDATE, merge-governance-check passing for exact head_sha
chain.current_status: EXTERNAL_POLICY — defined in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json but not enforced at GitHub settings level; depends on #380 closure

chain.name: canonical_main_commit
chain.description: The release is based on a commit that is reachable from the HEAD of the main branch, not a detached commit, feature branch commit, or pre-merge state.
chain.evidence_required: commit_sha present in main branch ancestry
chain.current_status: OPEN — no release workflow enforces main-branch origin check

chain.name: release_tag
chain.description: A git tag or GitHub release is created that references the canonical main commit. The tag must be immutable (annotated tag preferred). Tag overwrites, lightweight unsigned tags, and tags pointing to non-main commits are not canonical.
chain.evidence_required: tag name, tag SHA, tagged commit SHA, tag type (annotated/signed preferred)
chain.current_status: OPEN — no governed tag creation workflow; any writer can create or overwrite tags

chain.name: release_notes
chain.description: Release notes reference the canonical commit SHA, the PR(s) merged, and the validation evidence (PREO_VALID, status checks). Release notes alone are not proof — they must bind to traceable evidence.
chain.evidence_required: reference to commit_sha, PR number(s), PREO_VALID artifact reference, status check run IDs
chain.current_status: OPEN — no release notes template or governance requirement for evidence binding

chain.name: provenance_attestation_reference
chain.description: The release binds a provenance or attestation reference: a DSSE attestation, SLSA provenance document, or equivalent signed metadata referencing the commit SHA and artifact hash. Release provenance is evidence-only unless later bound by explicit authority.
chain.evidence_required: provenance document URL or hash, attestation type (DSSE/SLSA/equivalent), signer identity
chain.current_status: OPEN — no signed attestation or SLSA provenance workflow exists; classified OPEN pending implementation

chain.name: immutable_artifact_identity
chain.description: The release artifact (build output, package, container image) has an immutable cryptographic identity: a SHA-256 or stronger hash that binds the artifact bytes to the release tag and provenance reference. Artifact rebuild drift, hash mismatch, or missing hash disqualifies the artifact.
chain.evidence_required: artifact_hash (SHA-256 or stronger), hash algorithm, artifact name and version
chain.current_status: OPEN — no artifact hash generation or verification workflow; package.json private=true, no publish workflow exists

classification_schema.CANONICAL_RELEASE: A release that satisfies all seven steps in the canonical_release_boundary: PR-reviewed commit → required status checks → canonical main commit → release tag → release notes with evidence → provenance/attestation reference → immutable artifact identity. Currently no release can be classified CANONICAL_RELEASE due to the OPEN gaps in the boundary.
classification_schema.NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE: A release that was created but does not satisfy the canonical boundary — created from a non-main commit, unreviewed commit, without status check evidence, or without provenance/attestation binding. Such releases exist as repository artifacts but are not legitimate governance artifacts.
classification_schema.OPEN: A release-capable path that is currently uncontrolled at the platform level. No active enforcement mechanism exists. Represents a live provenance gap that must be resolved for release governance closure.
classification_schema.EXTERNAL_POLICY: A release governance control that is defined in policy artifacts but depends on a human administrator applying it at the GitHub repository settings level, or on an external signing infrastructure not yet deployed. The control is documented but not currently active.
classification_schema.BREAK_GLASS: A release path that exists as a root authority capability — admin/owner can create any release at any time. Cannot be eliminated at the repository level. Classified as non-normal execution. A BREAK_GLASS release cannot create MindShift execution legitimacy and must be recorded as an explicit break-glass event.
classification_schema.EVIDENCE_ONLY: A release path that is observable but not enforceable in repository code. The path's existence and outcome can be recorded for audit but cannot be blocked by repository-level governance. Closure requires external infrastructure (signing keys, attestation services, registry policies).

provenance_paths.description: Creation of a git tag or GitHub release by any repository writer without canonical chain traversal, PREO validation, or proof lineage. Any writer with push access can create a tag against any commit.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No governed release or tag creation workflow exists. RCM-016 in REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json: OPEN, linked #382. No signed-tag requirement. No provenance attestation boundary defined.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. GitHub allows any repository writer to create tags and releases. No platform-level constraint on tag target commit or provenance.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Tags and GitHub releases can be created without canonical chain traversal, PREO validation, or proof lineage. Any repository contributor with push access can tag any commit as a release. Release artifacts cannot be traced back to governed repository state.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Implement a governed release workflow that: (1) verifies the tagged commit is reachable from main HEAD, (2) verifies the commit was merged via a reviewed PR with passing status checks, (3) generates a provenance artifact binding the release tag to the commit SHA and artifact hash, (4) records the release in an evidence registry. Require repository collaborator restriction so only authorized accounts have push access.

provenance_paths.description: Creation of a lightweight (unannotated) or unsigned git tag that lacks cryptographic provenance. Unsigned tags can be created, moved, or deleted without trace. The tag target commit cannot be verified as authored by a specific identity.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No signed-tag requirement exists in BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json or GitHub repository settings. No GPG/SSH signing requirement for tags. Lightweight tags are accepted by default.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. GitHub does not enforce annotated or signed tag creation. Repository settings have no require_signed_tags option at the repository level (only available at organization level via rulesets).
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Lightweight or unsigned tags provide no cryptographic evidence of who created the tag, when, or against which verified commit. Tag impersonation and unsigned provenance are possible for any repository writer.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Enforce annotated tag creation in any governed release workflow. Require tag signing via GPG or SSH when feasible. Classify all unsigned release tags as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE pending signing infrastructure. Document as OPEN until signing enforcement is active.

provenance_paths.description: Overwriting an existing git tag (force-push of tag reference) to point to a different commit. Tag mutation erases the original provenance binding and makes the release artifact non-deterministic.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No tag protection rules are defined in GitHub repository settings. Tag overwrites are allowed by default. BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json does not include tag protection scope.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None at repository level. GitHub repository rulesets can protect tag patterns but are not configured. No tag immutability constraint exists.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Any writer with push access can force-push a tag to point to a different commit, silently replacing provenance. No audit trail is generated in repository governance artifacts. The GitHub release associated with the tag continues to serve the new (overwritten) commit.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Configure GitHub repository tag protection rules to prevent tag deletion and overwrite for governed release tag patterns (e.g., v*). Enforce via GitHub rulesets (EXTERNAL_POLICY until configured). Add tag immutability check in governed release workflow.

provenance_paths.description: GitHub release notes that do not reference any validation evidence — no PREO_VALID artifact, no status check run IDs, no PR reference, no commit SHA binding. Release notes describe the release in prose but are not traceable to governed repository state.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release notes template or requirement for evidence binding exists. GitHub release notes are free-form text. No governance check validates release note content.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Release notes are user-authored text with no structural requirement.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Release notes without evidence binding cannot be used to verify the release was produced from a governed commit. Auditors cannot reconstruct the governance chain from the release alone.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Define a release notes template that requires: tagged commit SHA, merge PR number(s), PREO_VALID artifact reference or status check run IDs, artifact hash, provenance document reference. Validate template compliance in governed release workflow.

provenance_paths.description: A release artifact (build output, binary, or package) published without a provenance reference — no SLSA provenance document, no DSSE attestation, and no artifact hash bound to the release metadata.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No artifact build workflow exists. package.json private=true with no publish configuration. No provenance generation step defined in any workflow. No attestation service configured.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Artifact provenance generation is not implemented.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: If an artifact were published (e.g., as a GitHub release asset or npm package), it would have no cryptographic provenance binding it to the source commit, build environment, or governance evidence. Artifact substitution and supply chain compromise would be undetectable.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Implement artifact provenance generation using SLSA Level 1 minimum: record builder identity, source URI, commit SHA, artifact hash. Attach provenance document to GitHub release. Classify as OPEN until implementation is complete.

provenance_paths.description: Publication of an npm package (or equivalent artifact registry entry) without verifying the package source commit is canonical — i.e., reachable from main HEAD and merged via reviewed PR with status check evidence.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No npm publish workflow exists. package.json has no publish configuration and private=true. No package publication governance policy defined. RCM-017 in REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json: OPEN, linked #382.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. package.json private=true acts as a passive deterrent but does not prevent manual npm publish with registry credentials.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A developer with npm credentials and registry access could publish a package from any local source tree, including uncommitted changes, unreviewed branches, or non-main commits. The published artifact would have no traceable link to governed repository state.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Define package publication governance policy: (1) publication only from governed release workflow, (2) require canonical source commit verification before publish, (3) require npm provenance flag (--provenance) when publishing, (4) restrict npm token to GitHub Actions OIDC. Classify as OPEN until implementation.

provenance_paths.description: Creation of a GitHub release targeting a commit that is not reachable from the main branch HEAD — e.g., a release from a feature branch, a detached HEAD, a pre-merge commit, or a tag pointing to a different branch's history.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release workflow enforces main-branch origin check. GitHub UI allows release creation targeting any tag or commit. No branch-of-origin constraint exists.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. GitHub does not restrict releases to commits reachable from a specific branch.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A release created from a non-main commit bypasses the entire PR review and status check governance chain. The tagged commit may have never been reviewed, tested, or classified by PREO/SCO workflows.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Governed release workflow must verify that the release target commit SHA is reachable from git rev-list main. Reject release creation for commits not in main ancestry. Classify releases from non-main commits as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE.

provenance_paths.description: Creation of a release from a commit that was merged to main without CODEOWNERS review or without required status checks — e.g., via direct push, admin bypass, or merge before branch protection was enforced.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release workflow verifies the source commit's PR review provenance. BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json requires CODEOWNERS review and status checks but is not enforced at GitHub settings level (#380 open). Release creation does not check PR review evidence.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None at release creation time. Depends on #380 closure for upstream commit review enforcement.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A commit merged without CODEOWNERS review (via direct push or admin bypass) is in the main branch history and can be tagged for release. The release would appear legitimate but the source commit lacks the required governance evidence. Closure depends on both #380 (commit review enforcement) and #382 (release provenance verification).
provenance_paths.closure_action: Governed release workflow must verify that the tagged commit was merged via a PR with CODEOWNERS approval and passing status checks, by checking GitHub API PR review state and check run evidence for the commit SHA. Classify releases from unreviewed commits as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE.

provenance_paths.description: A release created without verifying that the source commit has associated status check evidence — specifically that merge-governance-check, generate-preo-candidate, and generate-sco-candidate all passed for the exact commit SHA being released.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release workflow checks GitHub Actions status check evidence for the release commit. PREO_VALID is a merge gate only if branch protection required_status_checks are enforced (#380 open).
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None at release creation time. Status checks run but are not verified at release time.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A release tag could be created against a commit where merge-governance-check failed, PREO was invalid, or status checks were skipped. The release would have no verifiable validation evidence binding.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Governed release workflow must query GitHub API check runs for the release commit SHA, verify merge-governance-check and generate-preo-candidate both passed, and attach the check run IDs to the provenance artifact. Fail release creation if status check evidence is missing or failed.

provenance_paths.description: A GitHub release published without a cryptographic hash of the release artifact(s) — no SHA-256 or stronger hash included in the release notes, provenance document, or release asset manifest.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No artifact build or hash generation workflow exists. No release notes requirement for artifact hash. No release asset manifest with checksums.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Artifact hashes are not generated or required.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Without artifact hashes, consumers cannot verify that a downloaded artifact matches the artifact produced from the tagged commit. Artifact substitution during distribution would be undetectable. Artifact rebuild drift would produce different artifacts with the same version label.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Generate SHA-256 hashes of all release artifacts in the governed release workflow. Attach a checksums file (SHA256SUMS or equivalent) as a release asset. Include artifact_hash in the provenance document. Verify artifact_hash in attestation before release publication.

provenance_paths.description: A release created without any reference to the deployment proof registry — no deployment_proof_registry entry, no proof_binding_hash, no proof lineage connecting the released artifact to a governed deployment event.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release workflow queries or references the deployment_proof_registry. Release creation is entirely independent of the canonical deployment chain (/session → /proof). No proof_binding_hash is required for release creation.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Release and deployment are disconnected surfaces.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Releases exist independently of the deployment provenance chain. A release artifact may never have been deployed through the governed chain, or the deployed artifact may differ from the released artifact. No bi-directional binding between release and deployment proof exists.
provenance_paths.closure_action: When a governed deployment exists for the release commit, include a reference to the deployment_proof_registry entry (proof_binding_hash) in the provenance document. Releases without deployment proof linkage are classified EVIDENCE_ONLY for that field — deployment linkage is not required for release legitimacy but is required for full canonical traceability.

provenance_paths.description: A developer creates a tag locally (git tag v1.0.0 <sha>) and pushes it directly to the repository (git push origin v1.0.0) without going through any governed release workflow. The tag appears in GitHub as a valid release reference.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No tag creation restriction exists at the GitHub repository settings level. Any collaborator with push access can push a tag directly. No governed release workflow is required for tag creation.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Direct tag push is equivalent to direct code push for GitHub's permission model.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Local tag push bypasses all release provenance checks, validation evidence binding, and artifact hash generation. Any contributor with push access can create a release tag without governance review, making the release look legitimate in GitHub without provenance backing.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Configure GitHub repository tag protection via rulesets to require a governed workflow for tag creation (EXTERNAL_POLICY once configured). Until rulesets are active, classify all locally-pushed tags as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE pending provenance verification. Document as OPEN.

provenance_paths.description: Creating a GitHub release via the GitHub web UI (Releases → Draft a new release) which bypasses any workflow-based release governance. The GitHub UI allows release creation targeting any tag, any commit, and any branch without workflow validation.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No repository-level restriction on GitHub UI release creation. GitHub UI release creation is an unconditional platform capability for any repository collaborator with triage or write access. Cannot be disabled at the repository level without GitHub Enterprise rulesets.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Cannot be restricted at the repository level without GitHub Enterprise organization rulesets (EXTERNAL_POLICY). Repository-level governance cannot prevent GitHub UI release creation by authorized collaborators.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: GitHub UI release creation is an irreducible platform capability for any repository collaborator with write access. Repository governance cannot block this path at the code level. Any UI-created release must be classified NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE and requires post-hoc provenance verification to achieve canonical status.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Classify all GitHub UI-created releases as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE pending provenance review. Require that all canonical releases reference a governed workflow run ID in the release notes. Enforce restricting collaborator access to named human accounts (EXTERNAL_POLICY). Document as EXTERNAL_POLICY — no repository-level code change can eliminate this path.

provenance_paths.description: A GitHub Actions workflow that creates a release or tag automatically — e.g., on: push to main or on: workflow_dispatch — without full canonical chain verification. Currently no release workflow exists; this path is prospective.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No release creation workflow exists in .github/workflows/. No automated release or tag creation workflow is defined. This path is prospective — it would exist if a release workflow were introduced without governance controls.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: No enforcement needed currently (no such workflow exists). If introduced, the workflow must: (1) verify the tagged commit is on main, (2) verify PR review evidence, (3) verify status check evidence, (4) generate provenance artifact. Until then, observable only via workflow file monitoring.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE if introduced without governance controls
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A future release workflow that lacks canonical chain verification would create ungoverned releases at scale. constitutional-integrity.yml monitors for new workflow introductions (workflow_mutation class in PREO/SCO classification). Closure requires that any release workflow introduced after #382 include the full provenance verification chain.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Monitor for introduction of release workflows via constitutional-integrity.yml. Any release workflow introduced must include all steps of the canonical release boundary. Classify as EVIDENCE_ONLY — observable via workflow monitoring but not enforceable until a governed release workflow is implemented.

provenance_paths.description: An automated bot account or GitHub App with write access creating a release or tag — e.g., a release bot, changelog generator, or CI bot — without human review of the provenance chain.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No bot-specific restriction on release creation exists in repository governance artifacts. Repository collaborator settings control bot access but no bot-specific policy is defined for release creation. CODEOWNERS review enforcement depends on branch protection (#380 open).
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Depends on GitHub repository collaborator settings and organization policy. No bot-specific release governance constraint is defined within repository governance artifacts. Enforcement requires restricting bot accounts from release creation at the organization or repository level.
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Bots or GitHub Apps with write access could create releases without human CODEOWNERS review. Bot-created releases would not be classified as canonical releases. Classification depends on collaborator access control at the GitHub organization/repository level, external to repository governance code.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Restrict repository collaborator access to named human accounts only (EXTERNAL_POLICY). Require all bot access to releases be via a governed workflow with explicit human approval gate. Classify all bot-created releases as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE pending human provenance review. Document as EXTERNAL_POLICY.

provenance_paths.description: A GitHub repository administrator or organization owner creating a release or tag by overriding any future tag protection rules, bypassing branch protection requirements, or using root credential access (CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, GitHub admin credentials) to publish artifacts outside the governed release workflow.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No tag protection rules are currently enforced. GitHub repository administrators have platform-level authority to create any release against any commit. Root credentials (GitHub admin credentials, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN) are classified BREAK_GLASS in REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json (RCM-019). Cannot be eliminated at the repository level.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: Cannot be restricted against GitHub platform root authority. GitHub repository admin has irrevocable platform-level capability to create releases and bypass tag protections. Classified as non-normal execution path requiring explicit audit log entry.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE — admin-created releases cannot be classified CANONICAL_RELEASE through MindShift governance
provenance_paths.residual_gap: GitHub repository administrator has platform-level authority that cannot be constrained by repository-level governance artifacts. Even with tag protection rules enforced, admin bypass remains a root authority capability. This gap cannot be closed at the repository level; it is classified and acknowledged as BREAK_GLASS. All admin-created releases must be documented as explicit break-glass events.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Enforce allow_admin_bypass=false for tag protection rules (EXTERNAL_POLICY once rulesets are configured). Document all admin release events as explicit break-glass incidents with GitHub audit log evidence. Periodic audit of GitHub audit log for admin release creation. Classify as BREAK_GLASS — irrevocable at repository level.

provenance_paths.description: Rebuilding a release artifact from the same source commit at a later time produces a different binary (non-deterministic build), causing the artifact hash to differ from the original release artifact hash. The rebuilt artifact has the same version label but different immutable identity.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No build determinism requirement is defined for this project. No build reproducibility verification exists. No artifact hash verification workflow exists. Package build tooling (Node.js, TypeScript) may produce non-deterministic output.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Build reproducibility is not enforced.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE — rebuilt artifact with different hash is a different artifact, not the canonical release artifact
provenance_paths.residual_gap: If release artifacts are rebuilt (e.g., for hotfix, re-distribution, or CI retry), the rebuilt artifact may differ from the originally published artifact due to build-time non-determinism (timestamps, dependency resolution, etc.). Without artifact hash verification, rebuild drift is undetectable.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Lock all build dependencies to exact versions in package-lock.json (already present but verify determinism). Define artifact hash verification: record SHA-256 of original build artifact, compare against rebuild. Classify artifact rebuild drift as a provenance invalidation event. Document as OPEN until build determinism is verified.

provenance_paths.description: Creation of a rollback release (re-releasing a previous version, re-tagging a prior commit, or reverting to a prior state as a new release) without verifying that the rollback commit still satisfies the canonical release boundary at the time of rollback.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No rollback release governance policy exists. The deployment_rollback_registry tracks deployment rollback events (RCM-015 CLOSED) but has no binding to release provenance. A rollback release could re-tag a commit whose original governance evidence has since been superseded or invalidated.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Rollback releases are ungoverned.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE — rollback releases require fresh provenance verification at rollback time
provenance_paths.residual_gap: A rollback release re-introduces an old artifact version that may have been superseded by security patches or governance changes. The original commit's PR review and status check evidence is historical and may not reflect the current governance posture. No rollback release governance policy is defined.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Define rollback release governance policy: (1) rollback target commit must still be in main branch history, (2) original PR review evidence must be documented, (3) rollback release must reference the deployment_rollback_registry entry if applicable, (4) a new release provenance artifact must be generated at rollback time. Classify as OPEN pending policy implementation.

provenance_paths.description: Reusing an existing release provenance document or attestation artifact to legitimize a different release — attaching a valid provenance document from release v1.0.0 to the release metadata for v1.0.1, making v1.0.1 appear to have canonical provenance it does not have.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No provenance document schema or uniqueness enforcement exists. No provenance registry exists. No attestation verification workflow exists that would detect provenance document reuse across releases.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Provenance replay detection is not implemented.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE — replayed provenance does not constitute valid attestation for the target release
provenance_paths.residual_gap: Without a provenance registry and uniqueness enforcement, a provenance document from one release could be copied and attached to a different release. The consuming party would see a valid-looking provenance artifact without being able to detect that it was generated for a different artifact version.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Bind provenance documents to the specific artifact hash and release tag via the subject field (SLSA pattern) or equivalent. Include a unique attestation_id or nonce in each provenance document. Maintain a provenance registry that maps (release_tag, artifact_hash) → provenance_document_hash to detect replay attempts. Classify as OPEN pending implementation.

provenance_paths.description: A release where the provenance attestation document references a different artifact hash than the artifact actually distributed — either due to artifact substitution after attestation, build environment drift, or attestation document tampering.
provenance_paths.current_gate: No attestation document verification workflow exists. No artifact hash verification at distribution time. No attestation signing or integrity check is defined.
provenance_paths.enforcement_mechanism: None. Attestation mismatch detection is not implemented.
provenance_paths.non_canonical_outcome: NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE — attestation mismatch disqualifies the release from canonical status
provenance_paths.residual_gap: An attestation mismatch could occur from: (1) artifact substitution after the provenance document was generated, (2) build environment non-determinism producing a different artifact than the one attested, (3) attestation document tampering by a third party. Without verification, mismatches are undetectable at distribution time.
provenance_paths.closure_action: Implement attestation verification: at release time, recompute the artifact hash and compare against the provenance document subject hash. Sign the provenance document to prevent tampering. Verify attestation integrity before publishing the release. Classify as OPEN until attestation verification is implemented.

governance_invariants.statement: release capability does not equal release legitimacy
governance_invariants.description: The ability to create a GitHub release or git tag (having repository write access) grants no authority over the governance status of that release. A release is only CANONICAL_RELEASE if all seven steps of the canonical_release_boundary are satisfied. Tag creation without canonical boundary compliance is a NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE regardless of the actor's permissions.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-001 creates_release_legitimacy=false; RPM-012 creates_release_legitimacy=false; REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json RCM-016 creates_legitimacy=false; canonical_release_boundary step 4 release_tag current_status=OPEN

governance_invariants.statement: tag existence does not equal canonical release
governance_invariants.description: The existence of a git tag or GitHub release in the repository does not constitute a canonical release. A tag must be traced through the full canonical release boundary — PR-reviewed commit, status check evidence, main branch origin, provenance reference, and immutable artifact identity — to be classified CANONICAL_RELEASE. An unverified tag is NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE or OPEN pending verification.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-002 unsigned_tag_creation creates_release_legitimacy=false; RPM-003 mutable_tag_overwrite creates_release_legitimacy=false; RPM-007 github_release_from_non_main_commit non_canonical_outcome=NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE; classification_schema CANONICAL_RELEASE requires all 7 boundary steps satisfied

governance_invariants.statement: artifact existence does not equal provenance
governance_invariants.description: The existence of a build artifact, release asset, or npm package does not constitute provenance. Provenance requires a verifiable binding between the artifact's cryptographic hash and a signed document tracing the build to a specific source commit, build environment, and governance evidence chain. An artifact without a bound provenance document is EVIDENCE_ONLY at best and NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE in the absence of governance tracing.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-005 artifact_without_provenance_reference creates_release_legitimacy=false; RPM-010 release_without_artifact_hash non_canonical_outcome=NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE; RPM-020 attestation_mismatch non_canonical_outcome=NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE; canonical_release_boundary step 6 provenance_attestation_reference current_status=OPEN

governance_invariants.statement: release notes do not equal proof
governance_invariants.description: Release notes are prose documentation of a release and do not constitute governance proof. Even when release notes reference commit SHAs, PR numbers, and status checks, they are not cryptographically signed, not append-only, and can be edited after the fact. Release notes without bound validation evidence are NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE markers. Only a signed provenance document bound to an immutable artifact hash constitutes proof-level evidence for release governance.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-004 release_notes_without_validation_evidence creates_release_legitimacy=false; classification_schema NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE applies to releases without provenance binding; canonical_release_boundary step 5 requires evidence reference in notes AND step 6 provenance attestation — notes alone are insufficient

governance_invariants.statement: a canonical release must bind tag, commit SHA, artifact hash, validation evidence, status check evidence, reviewer/CODEOWNERS evidence when available, provenance reference, and release timestamp
governance_invariants.description: The CANONICAL_RELEASE classification requires all eight fields to be present and bound to each other: (1) release tag name, (2) tagged commit SHA, (3) artifact hash (SHA-256 or stronger), (4) validation evidence (PREO_VALID reference or equivalent), (5) status check evidence (check run IDs for merge-governance-check and generate-preo-candidate), (6) reviewer/CODEOWNERS evidence when available (PR review record, reviewer identity), (7) provenance reference (signed document or SLSA provenance), (8) release timestamp. Missing any binding renders the release NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE.
governance_invariants.basis: canonical_release_boundary chain steps 1–7 define required bindings; RPM-004 through RPM-020 classify paths that fail individual binding requirements as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE or OPEN; no current release satisfies all 7 canonical boundary steps; classification_schema CANONICAL_RELEASE definition

governance_invariants.statement: release and tag creation cannot create authority
governance_invariants.description: Creating a release tag or GitHub release grants no authority to execute, deploy, or mutate production state through the MindShift canonical chain. Release creation is a repository annotation action. Authority in MindShift requires /session → /continuity → /authority traversal. A release tag does not substitute for or accelerate authority creation. release_creates_authority=false for all classified provenance paths.
governance_invariants.basis: All 20 RPM paths have creates_authority=false except RPM-016 (BREAK_GLASS admin path where authority_capable=true reflects root platform capability, not MindShift governance authority); REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json RCM-016 authority_capable=false; RCM-017 authority_capable=false; root_authority_constraints.json root_authority_escalation=NULL

governance_invariants.statement: release provenance is evidence-only unless later bound by explicit authority
governance_invariants.description: A release provenance document (SLSA provenance, DSSE attestation, or equivalent) is an evidence artifact. It records facts about the build and release process but does not authorize any execution, deployment, or governance action. Provenance evidence must be bound to explicit authority through the MindShift canonical chain (/authority → /compile → /validate → /execute → /proof) to authorize a deployment. Provenance alone cannot substitute for a proof registry entry.
governance_invariants.basis: MUTATION_SURFACE_EXHAUSTIVENESS.json EVIDENCE_ONLY classification for evidence surfaces; RCM-015 deployment_provenance_proof_rollback_registries creates_legitimacy=false; canonical_release_boundary step 6 provenance_attestation_reference is a verification step, not an authority grant; RPM-011 release_without_deployment_proof_linkage closure_action specifies proof linkage as EVIDENCE_ONLY

governance_invariants.statement: admin and root release paths must be classified as BREAK_GLASS
governance_invariants.description: GitHub repository administrator authority, GitHub organization owner authority, and any account with bypass capability over tag protection rules or release governance controls are root-level authorities external to the MindShift governance model. All releases created via admin bypass must be classified BREAK_GLASS, documented as non-normal execution events, and recorded in the GitHub audit log. Admin-created releases cannot be classified CANONICAL_RELEASE.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-016 admin_root_release_bypass classification=BREAK_GLASS non_normal_execution=true creates_release_legitimacy=false; REPOSITORY_SOVEREIGNTY_MATRIX.json RSI-005: external GitHub admin and root authority must classify as BREAK_GLASS; REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json RCM-019 root_credential_break_glass BREAK_GLASS creates_legitimacy=false

governance_invariants.statement: non-main or unreviewed release paths must be classified as NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE or OPEN
governance_invariants.description: Any release created from a commit that is not reachable from the main branch HEAD, or from a commit that was merged without CODEOWNERS review or required status checks, must be classified NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE or OPEN. Non-main-origin releases and unreviewed releases cannot be upgraded to CANONICAL_RELEASE status without re-verification of the full canonical release boundary against a reviewed main-branch commit.
governance_invariants.basis: RPM-007 github_release_from_non_main_commit non_canonical_outcome=NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE; RPM-008 release_from_unreviewed_commit non_canonical_outcome=NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE; classification_schema NON_CANONICAL_RELEASE definition; REPOSITORY_SOVEREIGNTY_MATRIX.json RSI-004: unsigned and unreviewed mutation paths must classify as unauthorized

governance_invariants.statement: release provenance must not mutate validator, execution, proof, or authority semantics
governance_invariants.description: Release provenance artifacts (this matrix, the boundary document, the attestation infrastructure) are evidence-only artifacts. They classify and document the release governance boundary. They do not modify any runtime route, validator behavior, proof generation logic, execution surface, or authority creation mechanism. Release governance is a classification and evidence layer above the canonical chain, not a replacement or extension of it.
governance_invariants.basis: Scope constraint: evidence/tests/docs only; no runtime route changes; no validator changes; no proof behavior changes; no authority creation; no deployment capability expansion. RPM-001 through RPM-020 all have creates_authority=false (except BREAK_GLASS RPI-006 note); canonical_release_boundary is a verification specification, not an execution gate in the MindShift runtime

summary.note_on_canonical_release_satisfiability: No release can currently be classified CANONICAL_RELEASE. All seven steps of the canonical_release_boundary have at least one OPEN or EXTERNAL_POLICY dependency. The primary blockers are: (1) no governed release workflow exists (RPM-001 OPEN), (2) branch protection not enforced (#380 open, RPM-008), (3) no provenance/attestation workflow exists (RPM-005, RPM-006 OPEN), (4) no artifact hash generation exists (RPM-010 OPEN). Closure of all OPEN paths requires implementation of a governed release workflow that satisfies all boundary steps.
summary.rcm_016_status_update: RCM-016 (release_tag_creation) was OPEN in REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json. This matrix defines the boundary and classifies all 20 release-capable paths. RCM-016 and RCM-017 remain OPEN pending governed release workflow implementation. The open gap is now fully classified — every release-capable path is declared, linked to evidence, and assigned a closure status.
summary.rcm_017_status_update: RCM-017 (package_artifact_publication) was OPEN in REVERSE_CLOSURE_MUTATION_MAP.json. RPM-006 (package_publication_without_canonical_source_commit) and RPM-005 (artifact_without_provenance_reference) classify the artifact publication surface as OPEN. Package publication governance requires a governed publish workflow, canonical source commit verification, and npm provenance flag.

residual_open_gaps.closure_requires: Implement governed release workflow with canonical boundary verification
GitHub repository tag protection rules (EXTERNAL_POLICY once configured)
SLSA provenance or DSSE attestation workflow implementation
Governed release workflow + #380 branch protection enforcement
GitHub Enterprise organization rulesets (EXTERNAL_POLICY); cannot be eliminated at repository level
BREAK_GLASS classification only; cannot be eliminated at repository level

summary.issue_382_closure_condition: #382 can close only if every release-capable path is declared, linked to evidence, and assigned a closure status. This matrix satisfies that requirement — all 20 paths are declared and classified. Remaining external/root gaps (RPGAP-005, RPGAP-006) are explicit, not hidden. Full CANONICAL_RELEASE enforcement requires implementing the governed release workflow (RPGAP-001), provenance generation (RPGAP-003), and tag protection rules (RPGAP-002). Those are tracked as OPEN with explicit closure actions and do not block #382 closure, which requires classification completeness, not enforcement completeness.
